title: Hook output formatting (src/output.rs)
description: Format hook responses for Claude Code / Gemini / Copilot protocols. Uses Decision type from src/types.rs (defined in scaffold). JSON stdout with permissionDecision deny/allow, reason, severity, confidence, remediation (suggest spine tool alternative). Colorful stderr warning when blocked. File: src/output.rs. Acceptance: unit tests for each protocol format, verify stderr includes spine tool suggestion, test allow response is silent on stderr.

title: Spine tool detection: authorized subprocess recognition
description: Detect spine tool subprocess invocations in Bash commands. Takes &[String] tool names as input — decoupled from config system (config populates at integration time in main.rs). When command matches '<spine-tool> [flags] <file>', return SpineMatch with tool name and target path. Does NOT decide allow/deny — the evaluator handles that (spine detection only runs after sensitivity is confirmed). Handle full paths (/usr/local/bin/shape), piped commands (shape data.csv | jq). File: src/spine.rs. Acceptance: unit tests for basic match, full path match, pipe match, non-spine command returns None.

title: Hook protocol handler: parse stdin JSON, detect protocol, extract tool type and file path
description: Parse JSON input from Claude Code (PreToolUse), Gemini CLI, and GitHub Copilot hook protocols. Auto-detect which protocol is in use. Extract tool_name (Read/Grep/Bash) and raw tool_input. Does NOT extract file paths (that's bd-35q/bd-1x0). Returns structured HookInput with tool type and raw args. File: src/hook.rs. Acceptance: unit tests parsing Claude, Gemini, Copilot JSON formats. Test with unknown protocol returns graceful error.

title: Utility subcommands: test, explain, scan, packs, config, doctor (src/cli.rs)
description: Add CLI subcommands to existing clap structure: 'veil test <path>' checks decision, 'veil explain <path>' shows matching pack/reason, 'veil scan <dir>' classifies directory, 'veil packs' lists packs, 'veil config' shows resolved config, 'veil audit' shows recent log entries, 'veil doctor' validates installation. File: extends src/cli.rs. Acceptance: each subcommand produces output, --json flag works.

title: Homebrew formula for cmdrvl/tap
description: Add veil formula to homebrew-tap repo. Same pattern as canon.rb, lock.rb, cmdrvl-hash.rb — platform-specific tarballs from GitHub Releases for macOS arm/intel and Linux arm/intel. bin.install 'veil', test block verifying --version.

title: Bash command path extraction (src/path_bash.rs)
description: Extract file paths from Bash tool inputs. Parse cat/head/tail/less/more/bat commands to extract target file paths. Handle quoted paths, multiple arguments, piped commands (cat file | grep). Ignore commands that don't read files (ls, git, cd). File: src/path_bash.rs. Does NOT include heredoc/inline-script detection (that's bd-2aj). Acceptance: unit tests for cat/head/tail, quoted paths, pipes, non-read commands return empty.

title: Scaffold Cargo project with CI, release profile, and rust-toolchain.toml
description: Set up the Rust project skeleton: Cargo.toml with dependencies (serde, serde_json, clap, memchr, aho-corasick, glob, fancy-regex, colored, dirs, chrono, toml, toml_edit), release profile (opt-level z, lto, strip), rust-toolchain.toml, src/main.rs, src/lib.rs, src/types.rs, LICENSE. Follow spine tool conventions. src/types.rs defines shared types used across modules: Decision (allow/deny + reason + confidence + remediation), HookInput (tool type + raw args + protocol), SensitivityResult (pack match + severity + confidence + directory_sensitive flag). These are data-only structs with no logic. Acceptance: cargo build succeeds, cargo fmt --check passes, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes, cargo test passes (zero tests OK), binary reads stdin JSON and exits 0 with allow response.

title: Bash command path parsing: heredoc and inline script detection
description: For Bash tool inputs, implement deep path extraction beyond simple cat/head/tail. Detect heredoc file reads (cat <<EOF with file redirects), python -c 'open(path)', node -e 'fs.readFileSync(path)', and other indirect file access patterns. Leverage dcg's existing heredoc.rs and ast_matcher.rs as reference for AST-based extraction. This is the hardest bypass vector to defend. Acceptance: golden file tests for each pattern type (heredoc, python -c, node -e, perl -e, ruby -e), false positive test ensuring safe bash commands (echo, mkdir, git) pass through, test for nested quoting edge cases.

title: Audit log writer (src/audit.rs)
description: Append access attempt records to JSONL audit log at ~/.local/state/veil/audit.jsonl (default, outside repo to avoid committing sensitive paths). Uses Decision type from src/types.rs (defined in scaffold). Each record: timestamp, tool type, path, decision, reason, sensitivity level, confidence, session_id. Create directory if missing. File: src/audit.rs. Acceptance: unit test writes and reads back a log entry, test default path is outside repo, test directory auto-creation.

title: Sensitivity pack registry with modular packs
description: Modular sensitivity packs following dcg's pack architecture. Takes &[Pack] as input — decoupled from config system (config populates at integration time in main.rs). Built-in packs: core.filesystem (.env, .ssh/, *.pem, *.key), core.credentials (API keys, tokens, DB URLs), data.tabular (CSV/TSV/parquet), data.xml (XML filings), data.database (SQLite, DuckDB, dumps), compliance.financial, compliance.pii. Each pack returns SensitivityResult from types.rs including a directory_sensitive flag — packs that only trigger inside protected directories (data.tabular, data.xml) set this flag to true, and the evaluator (bd-6ya) handles the directory context check. Globally-triggering packs (core.filesystem, data.database) set flag to false. File: src/packs/ module. Acceptance: unit tests for each pack matching expected paths. Test that data.tabular match returns directory_sensitive=true. Test that core.filesystem match returns directory_sensitive=false.

title: Layered TOML configuration system
description: Implement config.rs with 5-layer configuration: env vars > project .veil.toml > user ~/.config/veil/config.toml > system /etc/veil/config.toml > compiled defaults. Support sensitivity patterns, allowlists, policy settings, spine tool registry, audit config. Partial layer merging (only override explicitly set fields). Model after dcg's config.rs. Acceptance: unit tests for each layer individually, test layer precedence (env overrides project overrides user), test missing files gracefully ignored, test env var VEIL_PROTECTED overrides config, test invalid TOML produces clear error.

title: CLI subcommands: test, explain, scan, packs, doctor, install
description: Implement CLI with clap: 'veil test <path>' checks if file would be blocked, 'veil explain <path>' shows why sensitive, 'veil scan <dir>' classifies directory contents, 'veil packs' lists sensitivity packs, 'veil config' shows resolved config, 'veil audit' shows recent log, 'veil doctor' self-tests, 'veil install/uninstall' manages settings.json hooks. Model after dcg's cli.rs.
close_reason: Split into core CLI and utility subcommands

title: Path hardening: symlink resolution, traversal attacks, canonicalization
description: Harden path handling against bypass attempts. New file: src/path_hardening.rs — called after path extraction (from 35q/1x0) and before matching (allowlist/packs). Resolve symlinks to real paths before matching, detect and block ../ traversal out of safe directories, canonicalize paths for consistent matching, handle /dev/fd/ and /proc/self/fd/ redirects. Edge cases: paths with spaces, unicode, null bytes. Acceptance: test symlink to protected file is blocked, test ../../../etc/passwd traversal is blocked, test /dev/fd/ redirect is blocked, test null byte injection is blocked, test paths with spaces and unicode are handled correctly, test non-existent symlink target fails gracefully.

title: CI workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml)
description: Set up GitHub Actions CI: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test. Trigger on push to main and PRs. File: .github/workflows/ci.yml. Acceptance: CI passes on empty scaffold.

title: Path extraction from Read, Grep, and Bash tool inputs
description: Implement path extraction for each tool type: Read tool has file_path field, Grep tool has path field, Bash tool requires parsing cat/head/tail/less/python -c/node -e commands to extract target file paths. Normalize relative paths to absolute. Handle quoted paths, glob arguments, piped commands. Model after dcg's context.rs span classification.
close_reason: Split into Read/Grep extraction and Bash extraction for parallelization

title: Installer script: curl | bash with platform detection
description: Create install.sh following dcg's installer pattern. Auto-detect platform (macOS arm/intel, Linux arm/intel), download pre-built binary from GitHub Releases, install to ~/.local/bin/veil, configure ~/.claude/settings.json with PreToolUse hooks for Read/Grep/Bash matchers. Support --uninstall, --status, --dry-run, --force. Idempotent.

title: Read/Grep path extraction (src/path_read_grep.rs)
description: Extract file paths from Read and Grep tool inputs. Read tool: parse file_path from tool_input JSON. Grep tool: parse path field + check output_mode (only 'content' mode leaks data; 'files_with_matches' is safe — allow it). Normalize relative paths to absolute using std::fs::canonicalize. ~30 lines of core logic. File: src/path_read_grep.rs. Acceptance: unit tests covering Read with absolute path, Read with relative path, Grep content mode (block), Grep files_with_matches mode (allow).

title: Test suite: unit, integration, golden file, and property tests
description: Comprehensive test coverage: unit tests for each module (path extraction, allowlist, sensitivity, spine detection, config), integration tests for end-to-end hook invocation with JSON stdin, golden file tests for known paths → expected decisions, property tests (allowlisted paths never denied, spine tools never denied, sensitive + no allowlist + no spine → always denied).
close_reason: Tests folded into module beads as acceptance criteria

title: Hook output: JSON response, stderr warning, and audit log
description: Implement output formatting: JSON stdout for hook protocol (permissionDecision deny/allow), colorful stderr warning explaining why access was blocked and suggesting spine tool alternatives, JSONL audit log appended to .veil/audit.jsonl with timestamp, tool type, path, decision, reason, sensitivity level, confidence, session ID. Model after dcg's output formatting.
close_reason: Split into output formatting and audit log

title: Core CLI framework and hook mode (src/cli.rs, src/main.rs)
description: Set up clap CLI with subcommand structure. Implement default (no subcommand) hook mode: read stdin JSON → parse via hook.rs → extract paths → evaluate → output response + audit log. Wire the full pipeline in main(). File: src/cli.rs + src/main.rs. Acceptance: cargo run with JSON stdin produces correct allow/deny output. --version works.

title: Policy engine: deny/warn/log resolution with confidence scoring
description: Unified evaluation entry point (src/evaluator.rs). Orchestrate: allowlist check (fast path) → sensitivity classification → if not sensitive ALLOW → if directory_sensitive, check protected dirs → spine tool check (Bash only, after sensitivity confirmed) → policy resolution. Takes all modules as trait objects or concrete types. Confidence scoring. Performance budget < 1ms, fail-open on timeout/error. File: src/evaluator.rs. Acceptance: integration test with mock module inputs: allowlisted path → allow, sensitive + spine tool → allow, sensitive + direct read → deny, non-sensitive → allow, directory_sensitive CSV outside protected dir → allow, timeout → allow with audit note.

title: GitHub Actions CI/CD: test, clippy, release workflow
description: Set up .github/workflows/ci.yml (cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy, cargo test) and release.yml (cross-compile for macOS arm/intel + Linux arm/intel, create GitHub Release with tarballs, trigger homebrew formula update). Follow spine tool CI patterns.
close_reason: Splitting into CI workflow (immediate) and release workflow (late)

title: Allowlist engine: safe pattern matching as fast path
description: Match file paths against safe patterns as fast-path allow. Takes &[Pattern] as input — decoupled from config system (config populates at integration time in main.rs). Compiled defaults: *.md, *.toml, *.lock, docs/**, tests/**, .github/**, *-report.json, *-report.yaml, package.json, tsconfig.json, Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock. NOTE: *.json and *.yaml are NOT in defaults (credentials.json, secrets.yaml are common sensitive files). Performance target: most tool invocations hit safe files, should allow in <200µs. File: src/allowlist.rs. Acceptance: unit tests for exact match, glob match, directory match. Test that credentials.json is NOT allowlisted by default.

title: Release workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml)
description: Cross-compile release binaries for macOS arm/intel + Linux arm/intel. Create GitHub Release with tarballs on tag push. Follow spine tool release patterns. File: .github/workflows/release.yml. Acceptance: tag push triggers release with 4 platform tarballs.
